_comment: R-requirement-claim-is-atomic / R-check-method-is-atomic ratchet baseline (Wave 8 move 2, 2026-07-03, EMPTIED). Starting from the 2026-07-02 RESCOPE (audit_atomicity.py's requirement-claim audit scoped to LIVE promises only -- status SETTLED or OPEN(...); REJECTED is frozen history per R-rejected-preserved-not-deleted, DRAFT is not yet a promise), the 6 remaining live compounds were resolved this wave: R-commit-boundary-checkable and R-tiered-gate-not-a-commit-gate/R-rules-as-data were false positives on the semicolon/em-dash scope-disclaimer heuristic (fixed via audit_atomicity._CLAIM_SCOPE_CLAUSE, wording reworded to the codebase's existing '--' disclaimer convention, no semantic change); R-observation-evidence-scope and R-subagent-gets-its-claude-md were genuine compounds where one half duplicated an existing sibling atom (R-no-observation-type, R-delegation-conclusions-only respectively) and were trimmed to their non-duplicate half; R-land-tier-trace was a genuine 3-way compound (record-shape+timing / dry-run-exemption / best-effort-on-write-failure, each independently tested) and was split into R-land-tier-trace + R-land-tier-trace-skips-dry-run + R-land-tier-trace-best-effort. Both requirements and invariants sets are now EMPTY -- R-requirement-claim-is-atomic and R-check-method-is-atomic flip to ENFORCED this wave, with test_atomicity_ratchet.py's two tests now acting as a STRICT zero-COMPOUND gate (any regression fails immediately, not just growth-beyond-a-nonzero-baseline). Growing this baseline again in the future is still the honest, permitted path if new compound debt needs to be carried rather than fixed immediately -- the ratchet mechanism (R-atomicity-ratchet-no-growth) does not require the baseline to stay empty, only that it never grows silently.
